search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae gastroenteritis adults telehealth follow-up red flags investigations
site:dha.gov.ae gastroenteritis adults history hydration referral 2024
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic persistent infection or post-infectious syndrome diagnosis
automatic rehydration, antibiotic, stool test, admission, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Persistent symptoms may have infectious and non-infectious causes.
No management or urgency is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults after presumed or confirmed gastroenteritis; children, pregnancy, immunocompromise, travel-related disease, severe dehydration, bleeding, and acute abdomen require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary, gastroenterology, hepatology, colorectal, result-review, or outpatient follow-up as qualified by the exact source.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE gastroenterology, colorectal, hepatology, laboratory, endoscopy, referral, prescribing, screening, and local-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited official source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA adult section supports interval diarrhoea, vomiting, abdominal symptoms, intake, hydration history, fever, blood, travel, exposure, medicine, and duration without confirming ongoing infection.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines acute diarrheal illness and records stool frequency, duration, nausea, vomiting, fever, abdominal pain, exposure pattern, and dehydration features.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact red-flag section supports clinician-assessed hydration, general status, and concern documentation without asserting absence.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists shock, severe dehydration, intractable vomiting, severe abdominal pain, prolonged symptoms, older age, comorbidity, pregnancy, antibiotic exposure, nocturnal diarrhea, and bloody or black stool.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact section supports recording existing laboratory or stool results without ordering tests.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes history-based assessment and investigation for suspected nonviral or complicated gastroenteritis.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact section supports clinician-entered safety net, escalation status, and follow-up without assigning urgency.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines emergency and face-to-face referral features for adult gastroenteritis.

unresolved_source_gaps: Original diagnosis, organism, current stool pattern, hydration, fever, bleeding, tests, diagnosis, treatment, return to work, referral, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
